message:
test(fuzz/session): add handshake round-trip invariants (br-abnue)

Close flywheel_connectors-abnue. The prior fuzz body for the session
handshake decoders only proved crash-resistance — it called
decode_hello_cbor / decode_ack_cbor / decode_cookie_bytes and discarded
every Ok without asserting any semantic property. A regression that
relaxed the canonical-CBOR gate (session.rs:469), or made
transcript_bytes nondeterministic, would have survived the fuzz run
while breaking every signed hello in production.

Added assertions, all scoped to the success branch so malformed inputs
still exercise the error paths:

  hello (decode_hello_cbor):
    - to_canonical_cbor(&hello) == data
      Pins the NORMATIVE canonical-decode gate — the decoder already
      re-encodes and compares internally (session.rs:452-474); the
      fuzz target now checks that the decoder's own contract holds.
    - Re-decode after canonical re-encode → byte-identical canonical
      projection. Detects any Serialize/Deserialize drift that skips
      the canonicality check.
    - transcript_bytes() is deterministic across repeat calls.
    - transcript begins with b"FCP2-HELLO-V1" — catches a regression
      that drops or renames the domain separator.

  ack (decode_ack_cbor):
    - to_canonical_cbor(&ack) == data, plus round-trip canonicality.
      Ack transcript derivation is covered transitively via hello
      because both use the same `append_cbor` helper.

  cookie (decode_cookie_bytes):
    - data.len() == SESSION_COOKIE_SIZE (32) on every Ok.
    - cookie.as_bytes() == data (identity — no in-line transformation).
    - Round-trip via decode_cookie_bytes(cookie.as_bytes()).

Verified:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-aux-p2-abnue cargo +nightly check \
    --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_session --offline
  -> Finished dev profile in 3m 14s with zero warnings in the target.

Closes flywheel_connectors-abnue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Idempotency-key behavior for side effects.

- Structured JSON logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a81.7","title":"E2E: Stripe Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Stripe Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Add Stripe scenarios to the shared compliance harness.

## Scenarios
- Default deny.
- Allow with valid token.
- Network guard allow/deny.
- Idempotency behavior for payment side effects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --package fcp-e2e --features stripe` passes deterministically.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a8xp","title":"TEST: Checkpoint/Frontier Unit Tests (Advancement, Fork Detection, Convergence)","description":"# TEST: Checkpoint/Frontier Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate ZoneCheckpoint/frontier advancement and fork detection semantics with deterministic unit/integration tests.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for checkpoint management and fork detection.

## Test Categories

### Checkpoint Advancement
- [ ] Normal advancement with quorum signatures
- [ ] Advancement blocked without quorum
- [ ] Checkpoint sequence monotonically increases
- [ ] Checkpoint binds AuditHead + RevocationHead
- [ ] Checkpoint signature verification

### Fork Detection
- [ ] Conflicting checkpoints detected
- [ ] Fork evidence is preserved
- [ ] Fork triggers alert/pause
- [ ] Fork resolution protocol
- [ ] No silent fork acceptance

### Convergence
- [ ] Nodes converge on same checkpoint
- [ ] Late-joining node catches up
- [ ] Partition recovery reaches consistency
- [ ] Checkpoint gossip propagation
- [ ] Convergence metrics tracking

### Frontier Management
- [ ] Frontier tracks latest valid checkpoint per zone
- [ ] Stale frontier detection
- [ ] Frontier update atomic with heads

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/checkpoint/`
- `checkpoint_valid.cbor`
- `checkpoint_fork_evidence.cbor`
- `convergence_scenario.json`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_fork_detection",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "zone_id": "z:work",
  "checkpoint_seq": 42,
  "fork_detected": true,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fork detection is reliable
- Convergence is achieved after partition heal
- All checkpoint operations are audit-logged

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-abnue","title":"fuzz/session: target decodes hello/ack/cookie bytes without invariants","description":"File: fuzz/fuzz_targets/session.rs:6-9

Finding: the fuzz body only calls `decode_hello_cbor(data)`, `decode_ack_cbor(data)`, and `decode_cookie_bytes(data)` and discards every result.

Why this matters: this only checks for panics. It never asserts transcript/session invariants when one or more decoders succeed, so semantic parser bugs can survive.

Suggested follow-up: when hello or ack decoding succeeds, assert stable transcript derivation / suite constraints / session-id shape rather than parse-and-drop only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:19:47.550495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:19:47.550495Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Why this matters: this only checks for panics. It never asserts transcript/session invariants when one or more decoders succeed, so semantic parser bugs can survive.

Suggested follow-up: when hello or ack decoding succeeds, assert stable transcript derivation / suite constraints / session-id shape rather than parse-and-drop only.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:19:47.550495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:42:46.421194Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:42:46.420905Z","close_reason":"Added semantic round-trip invariants to fuzz target: (1) decode_hello_cbor/decode_ack_cbor accepted inputs must canonically re-serialize byte-identical to the input (canonical CBOR gate at session.rs:469); (2) hello transcript_bytes is deterministic and carries FCP2-HELLO-V1 domain separator; (3) decode_cookie_bytes returns exactly 32 bytes and preserves input verbatim; (4) round-trip stability via re-decode. Compile verified with cargo +nightly check --bin fuzz_session.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-abwt","title":"TEST: Canonical Serialization Unit Tests (Deterministic CBOR, Schema Hash, CDDL Validation)","description":"# TEST: Canonical Serialization Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that serialization is deterministic and schema-bound.

## Test Categories

### Deterministic CBOR Encoding
- [ ] Same logical object → same bytes (always)
- [ ] Map key ordering is canonical (sorted)
- [ ] Integer encoding is minimal
- [ ] Float encoding is canonical
- [ ] No duplicate map keys
- [ ] Definite length encoding used

### Schema Hash Binding
- [ ] SchemaId (namespace, name, version) hashes correctly
- [ ] SchemaHash is 32 bytes (BLAKE3)
- [ ] Payload format: schema_hash || canonical_cbor_bytes
- [ ] Schema hash mismatch detected on decode

### Schema Validation
- [ ] Valid object passes schema check
- [ ] Missing required field rejected
- [ ] Wrong field type rejected
- [ ] Extra fields handled per policy (reject or ignore)
- [ ] Nested object validation works

### CDDL Compliance
- [ ] All FCP2 objects have CDDL definitions
- [ ] Generated bytes match CDDL constraints
- [ ] CDDL can be used for cross-language validation

### Roundtrip Tests
- [ ] Encode → decode → encode produces identical bytes
- [ ] All core object types roundtrip correctly
- [ ] Large objects roundtrip correctly
- [ ] Nested objects roundtrip correctly

### Bounds and Limits
- [ ] Oversized objects rejected at encode time
- [ ] Deeply nested objects bounded
- [ ] String length limits enforced
- [ ] Array length limits enforced

### Decode Safety
- [ ] Malformed CBOR rejected without panic
- [ ] Truncated input rejected
- [ ] Oversized length prefix rejected (DoS prevention)
- [ ] Recursive structure depth bounded

## Golden Vectors
- Each core object type with canonical bytes
- Schema hash computation examples
- Malformed inputs that must be rejected

## Property Tests
- Determinism: same input → same output
- Commutativity with schema: schema_hash(decode(encode(x))) == schema_hash(x)

## Fuzz Targets
- CBOR decode with arbitrary bytes
- Schema validation with malformed objects

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "object_type": "...",
  "schema_id": "...",
  "operation": "encode|decode|validate",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core types have golden vectors
- Determinism verified by property tests
- Fuzz targets find no panics

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","title":"fcp.airtable: Full CRUD operations on records","description":"# fcp.airtable: Full CRUD Operations on Records

## Goal
Implement record CRUD with batching, pagination, and strict safety controls.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.records.list` (pagination, filter/sort)
- `airtable.records.get`
- `airtable.records.create` / `airtable.records.create_batch`
- `airtable.records.update` / `airtable.records.update_batch`
- `airtable.records.upsert`
- `airtable.records.delete` / `airtable.records.delete_batch` (Dangerous)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write vs delete are separate capabilities
- Rate limiting and backpressure are mechanical.
- Pagination:
  - support continuation tokens
  - bound page sizes
- Batch operations:
  - handle partial failures deterministically
  - stable error mapping per record
- Idempotency / retries:
  - upsert is preferred for retry-safe workflows
  - deletes and schema writes must be treated as Dangerous (policy + receipts)

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - pagination edge cases
  - batch partial failure semantics
  - upsert conflict handling
  - redaction: never log record bodies by default

## Acceptance Criteria
- CRUD operations behave deterministically under retries.
- Dangerous operations are properly gated and audited.
","notes":"In-progress implementation slice completed: added Airtable batch update, upsert, and batch delete surfaces across client/connector/manifest/tests. Package-level verification passed via rch for fmt/check/test/clippy.

- new client helpers and response types added
- connector validation now enforces batch size and merge-field requirements for the new paths
- integration coverage added for client + connector invoke flows

Still pending for full bead closure:
- assess whether single-record CRUD should also resolve table refs through schema for stricter consistency
- consider deterministic partial-failure mapping / richer batch error surfaces if Airtable returns mixed mutation outcomes
- complete workspace-wide verification","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:33.751522695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.494549Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.494536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1054,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"ChartreuseLake","text":"Implemented Airtable batch update/upsert/delete plus tightened single-record and batch-create connector paths to resolve table refs through cached schema and validate fields payload shape. Added connector coverage for table-name resolution on create/create_batch/delete; package fmt/check/test/clippy are passing.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:23:28Z"},{"id":1055,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"ChartreuseLake","text":"Status after verification: keeping this bead IN_PROGRESS rather than closing it yet. The CRUD surface is now materially fuller and more consistent, but the acceptance text still explicitly calls out deterministic batch partial-failure semantics / stable per-record error mapping. Package-level fmt/check/test/clippy are green; workspace clippy remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing failures in connectors/box and connectors/asana.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:27:16Z"},{"id":1305,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented records.rs module: Record CRUD with builder pattern ListRecordsParams, batch operations (max 10), pagination (max 100), RecordValidator. 145 tests, all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g37n0","title":"fcp-slack: ok=true responses can panic on missing payload data","description":"## Finding
`SlackApiResponse<T>` stores the flattened success payload as `Option<T>` ([connectors/slack/src/types.rs:123-129](connectors/slack/src/types.rs:123)). `check_response()` only checks `ok` ([connectors/slack/src/client.rs:447-458](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:447)). Multiple client paths then call `resp.data.expect("ok response has data")` ([connectors/slack/src/client.rs:162](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:162), [209](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:209), [228](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:228), [238](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:238), [257](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:257), [270](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:270), [282](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:282), [302](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:302), [312](connectors/slack/src/client.rs:312)).

A malformed or partial `ok:true` body therefore becomes a process panic instead of an `FcpError`/`SlackError`. The crate already documents the `None` case in tests (`slack_api_response_error_has_no_data`), so this is not theoretical parser behavior.

## Repro shape
Return `{"ok":true}` or `{"ok":true,"channel":"C1"}` from `chat.postMessage`. `check_response()` accepts it and the subsequent `.expect(...)` aborts the connector.

## Recommendation
Replace each `.expect("ok response has data")` with an error-mapped `ok_or_else(...)` path that preserves the operation context and treats malformed success envelopes as API/protocol errors, not panics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:56:43.789357Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29.875248Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29.872787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2811,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g37n0","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in 2dd92d08. Root cause: 9 client paths called .expect("ok response has data") on a flattened Option<T> payload — a partial {"ok":true} envelope therefore panicked the connector instead of surfacing as an error. Fix: added Self::expect_data(data, method) helper that maps None to a terminal SlackError::Api { ok: true, error: "Slack API method \`X\` returned ok=true with no payload", .. }, and migrated all 9 call sites through it. The ok:true discriminator preserves the partial-envelope case as distinct from classic ok=false errors. Regression test ok_true_with_missing_payload_is_mapped_to_api_error_not_panic asserts the method name and 'ok=true' phrase appear in the error, and that to_fcp_error() maps to FcpError::External. Verified: 210 lib tests + 51/52 integration tests pass (the one pre-existing failure list_channels_happy_path is an unrelated URL-encoding bug confirmed by bisect).","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:23:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","title":"[FCP3/P3.2] Define canonical FCPS durable object schemas for runtime truth","description":"Background: a mesh first platform needs durable forms for the facts that survive process death and justify later decisions. Receipts, checkpoints, lease state, revocations, connector runtime state, and operator explanations cannot remain host local conventions.

Scope: define canonical FCPS object schemas for durable state, including connector identity and state, receipts, checkpoints, lease and fencing records, revocation records, and explanation bundles. Design them for replay, repair, inspection, and failover rather than as opaque storage details. Provide deterministic object examples, snapshot formats, explanation bundle shapes, schema unit tests, replay integration tests, and detailed inspection logs that later conformance and end to end harnesses can diff and diagnose.

Acceptance: the platform has a durable object vocabulary for the facts it must remember, those facts no longer depend on one host memory image to exist, and the schemas are concrete enough for fixture driven validation, replay, operator inspection, and regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.315492Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065699Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065467Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fcps","mesh-first","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2m2hl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.219178Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.654535Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2081,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Published FCPS durable object schemas at docs/FCP3_FCPS_Durable_Object_Schemas.md. Defines 7 object types: OperationReceipt (immutable execution proof), Checkpoint (state snapshot with lineage), Lease (exclusive ownership with fencing), Revocation (capability/session invalidation), ConnectorIdentity (deployment trust record), ExplanationBundle (human-readable decision justification), ConnectorState (durable runtime state). Common FcpsDurableObject header with schema versioning, content-addressing, and signatures. Includes fixtures, evolution rules, and inspection CLI commands.","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:08Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-gh0j7","title":"fuzz/fcps_frame: target only decodes frames/symbols without checking accepted-state invariants","description":"File: fuzz/fuzz_targets/fcps_frame.rs:18-37

Finding: the fuzz body only calls `FcpsFrameHeader::decode`, `FcpsFrame::decode`, `SymbolRecord::decode`, and `FrameFlags::from_bits_truncate`, then drops all successful results.

Why this matters: the target is good for panic discovery but weak for semantic coverage. It never checks that successfully decoded frames and symbol records obey internal size/header invariants.

Suggested follow-up: when decode succeeds, assert header/frame consistency, record bounds consistency, and stable behavior across repeated decode paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:20:10.749754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:20:10.749754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Why this matters: the target is good for panic discovery but weak for semantic coverage. It never checks that successfully decoded frames and symbol records obey internal size/header invariants.

Suggested follow-up: when decode succeeds, assert header/frame consistency, record bounds consistency, and stable behavior across repeated decode paths.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:20:10.749754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:39:00.595351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:39:00.594567Z","close_reason":"Verified: commit fe65bacc — fuzz/fcps_frame.rs now asserts (a) structural round-trip equality, (b) flags-truncation conditional header byte-equality, (c) length-field consistency, plus header-vs-frame agreement and encode determinism. Mirrors br-j5iby fcpc_frame pattern.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gh7gt","title":"fcp-raptorq: encode_all/into_encode_all ESI arithmetic uses wrapping add, not checked_add","description":"In `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs:141-144` (`encode_all`) and `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs:162-166` (`into_encode_all`), the repair symbol ESI is computed as `k_prime + i` where both are u32. In debug builds, overflow panics; in release, it wraps into the source-symbol ESI range and silently collides with existing source symbols.

**Bounded in practice.** `config.repair_symbols(source_symbols)` saturates to `u32::MAX`, but `source_symbols` comes from `payload_len.div_ceil(symbol_size)` where `payload_len ≤ config.max_object_size` (typically 64 MiB) and `symbol_size >= 1`. So `source_symbols ≤ 64 MiB`, well under u32::MAX. The current config bounds keep the arithmetic safe.

**Brittle.** The rest of the crate uses `saturating_add` / `checked_add` consistently (see `config.rs:155`, `decode.rs:585`, `decode.rs:598`). `encode.rs:142` and `encode.rs:164` are the only sites that use raw `+` on user-derived-ish arithmetic with no bound check.

**Suggested fix**: `let esi = k_prime.checked_add(i).ok_or(EncodeError::SymbolCountOverflow)?;` (or saturating_add). Mirror the `decode.rs:585` pattern.

Severity: LOW. Not currently exploitable; filed as a defensive-depth bead to keep the saturating/checked arithmetic discipline uniform across the crate.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:51.293708Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:43.532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:43.531732Z","close_reason":"Fix landed in c3cc8695 (saturate repair_count against k_prime overflow)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","title":"fcp.pinecone: Pinecone Vector Database Connector","description":"# fcp.pinecone - Pinecone Vector Database Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant connector for Pinecone (managed vector DB) supporting vector storage and semantic retrieval under strict zone isolation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `knowledge`

## Zone model
Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the configured Pinecone endpoint(s) and port(s) required by declared operations.

### Credential injection
- API key via `CredentialId`.
- Never persist or log keys.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `pinecone.index.manage`
- `pinecone.vectors.read`
- `pinecone.vectors.write`
- `pinecone.search.query`

## Operations (initial)
- index: create/describe/list/delete
- vectors: upsert/fetch/delete/query

## Tests
- Mock-only unit tests with wiremock.
- E2E compliance run validates default deny + NetworkConstraints + receipts for writes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner.
- Deterministic tests (no live Pinecone calls).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif.1","title":"fcp.pinecone: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.pinecone: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Pinecone connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`storage`, `knowledge`)
- operations + schema IDs
- capability families + per-operation caps
- NetworkConstraints allowlist bound to configured endpoint

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad manifest examples exist for static compliance.

@@ -1,10 +1,143 @@
+//! Session handshake fuzz target (br-abnue).
+//!
+//! Fuzzes the three wire-facing decoders on the session handshake:
+//!   - `decode_hello_cbor` (MeshSessionHello, canonical CBOR)
+//!   - `decode_ack_cbor`   (MeshSessionAck,   canonical CBOR)
+//!   - `decode_cookie_bytes` (SessionCookie, fixed 32-byte slice)
+//!
+//! Goals:
+//!   1. No panics on arbitrary input (crash-resistance).
+//!   2. **Semantic round-trip invariants** on accepted structures
+//!      (bead j5iby's sibling — br-abnue). The old body only called the
+//!      decoders and discarded every Ok; semantic parser regressions
+//!      (e.g. a future non-canonical accept-path, or transcript
+//!      derivation that becomes nondeterministic) would have survived.
+
 #![no_main]
 
-use fcp_protocol::{decode_ack_cbor, decode_cookie_bytes, decode_hello_cbor};
+use fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor;
+use fcp_protocol::{
+    SESSION_COOKIE_SIZE, decode_ack_cbor, decode_cookie_bytes, decode_hello_cbor,
+};
 use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
 
 fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
-    let _ = decode_hello_cbor(data);
-    let _ = decode_ack_cbor(data);
-    let _ = decode_cookie_bytes(data);
+    // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    // MeshSessionHello
+    // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    if let Ok(hello) = decode_hello_cbor(data) {
+        // Canonical-CBOR round-trip. `decode_canonical_cbor` in
+        // session.rs:452 explicitly re-encodes the decoded value with
+        // `to_canonical_cbor` and rejects inputs that don't match, so
+        // every accepted input MUST round-trip byte-for-byte. A
+        // regression that weakens the canonicality gate (e.g. relaxes
+        // from `canonical != bytes` to a semantic-equality check) would
+        // allow two distinct on-wire forms to decode to the same hello
+        // — that's exactly the parser-malleability class the NORMATIVE
+        // canonical gate exists to close.
+        let re_canonical = to_canonical_cbor(&hello)
+            .expect("canonically-decoded hello must re-serialize as canonical CBOR");
+        assert_eq!(
+            re_canonical, data,
+            "decode_hello_cbor accepted input differs from its canonical re-encoding",
+        );
+
+        // Redecode the re-encoded bytes and require structural
+        // equality: the public Serialize/Deserialize contract is what
+        // every session peer relies on.
+        let round_trip = decode_hello_cbor(&re_canonical)
+            .expect("canonically re-encoded hello must redecode");
+        // MeshSessionHello doesn't derive PartialEq, so compare via the
+        // canonical byte-projection (the same projection peers use to
+        // recognise the structure on the wire).
+        let round_trip_bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&round_trip)
+            .expect("round-tripped hello must re-canonicalize");
+        assert_eq!(
+            round_trip_bytes, re_canonical,
+            "decode(encode(hello)) must be canonically identical to hello",
+        );
+
+        // Transcript determinism. `transcript_bytes()` is what the
+        // signature is computed over and what `verify()` recomputes —
+        // two calls on the same struct MUST produce byte-identical
+        // transcripts or every signed hello breaks signature
+        // verification after a round-trip through the decoder.
+        let t1 = hello
+            .transcript_bytes()
+            .expect("hello transcript derivation must succeed on an accepted struct");
+        let t2 = hello
+            .transcript_bytes()
+            .expect("hello transcript derivation must be repeatable");
+        assert_eq!(
+            t1, t2,
+            "MeshSessionHello::transcript_bytes must be deterministic",
+        );
+
+        // Transcript carries the spec-level domain separation prefix.
+        // A regression that dropped or renamed the prefix would silently
+        // let an attacker craft a hello whose transcript collides with
+        // some other FCP2 message type's transcript.
+        assert!(
+            t1.starts_with(b"FCP2-HELLO-V1"),
+            "hello transcript must begin with FCP2-HELLO-V1 domain separator",
+        );
+    }
+
+    // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    // MeshSessionAck
+    // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    if let Ok(ack) = decode_ack_cbor(data) {
+        let re_canonical = to_canonical_cbor(&ack)
+            .expect("canonically-decoded ack must re-serialize as canonical CBOR");
+        assert_eq!(
+            re_canonical, data,
+            "decode_ack_cbor accepted input differs from its canonical re-encoding",
+        );
+
+        let round_trip = decode_ack_cbor(&re_canonical)
+            .expect("canonically re-encoded ack must redecode");
+        let round_trip_bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&round_trip)
+            .expect("round-tripped ack must re-canonicalize");
+        assert_eq!(
+            round_trip_bytes, re_canonical,
+            "decode(encode(ack)) must be canonically identical to ack",
+        );
+        // Ack's transcript requires a hello; the property we can assert
+        // without one is structural canonicality (above). Transcript-
+        // determinism is covered by the hello branch; the ack
+        // transcript code path reuses the same `append_cbor` helper.
+    }
+
+    // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    // SessionCookie — fixed 32-byte opaque tag
+    // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    if let Ok(cookie) = decode_cookie_bytes(data) {
+        // Cookies are raw 32-byte opaque tags. Any accepted input
+        // MUST be byte-identical to the cookie's internal bytes, and
+        // the decoder MUST reject anything other than exactly
+        // `SESSION_COOKIE_SIZE` bytes (see session.rs:227). The length
+        // assertion catches a regression that starts padding or
+        // truncating on the way in — the identity assertion catches
+        // any future in-line transformation (e.g. accidental
+        // re-hashing).
+        assert_eq!(
+            data.len(),
+            SESSION_COOKIE_SIZE,
+            "decode_cookie_bytes accepted input of the wrong length",
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            cookie.as_bytes().as_slice(),
+            data,
+            "decoded cookie bytes must equal the input bytes verbatim",
+        );
+
+        // Round-trip: re-decode from the cookie's own serialization.
+        let again = decode_cookie_bytes(cookie.as_bytes().as_slice())
+            .expect("cookie must decode from its own bytes");
+        assert_eq!(
+            again.as_bytes(),
+            cookie.as_bytes(),
+            "decode(cookie.as_bytes()) must equal cookie",
+        );
+    }
 });
